授業目的: 本講義は、政治学を初めて学ぶ受講生を対象に、政治学の学問背景や基本的な知識を学ぶことを目的とする。
到達目標: 以下の諸点を授業の目標として設定しています。・政治の世界を理解する為の学問的基盤を身につける。・クリティカルな思考と分析力を養成する。・政治学における基礎的な専門用語とコンセプトを習得する。
授業方法: This class will be conducted in a regular classroom setting face to face from the second class., while the first class will be held online.  There may be some class cancellations and make-up classes subsequently.  Make-up classes would be held on mutually convenient days/periods, which would be decided upon consultations between the lecturer and students.

トピック.授業外学習2: The number of study hours outside class would vary depending on students' background including their exposure to reading in English.  At least, one hour would be necessary to prepare for each class.

教科書/Required texts1: There will be no course readings to purchase. Required readings will be posted on LUNA. Major Japanese, American and European newspapers will be utilized as weekly readings.
学位授与の方針との関連Relationship to Diploma Polic: Each course is positioned within the curriculum in accordance with the diploma policy and curriculum policy established by each undergraduate/graduate school. By proceeding with the study of this course with an awareness of the DP and CP, the following abilities expected by each undergraduate/graduate school will be developed.General Foundation Courses (Basic Courses): 1. Understanding and analytical skills regarding international issues: the ability to understand and analyze various regions of the world from different perspectives in the humanities and social sciences.
成績評価Grading0: 評価基準等　Grading Criteria etc.
成績評価Grading2: プレゼンテーション・発表／Presentation (07)
成績評価Grading3: Comments/questions on required readings, class participation
成績評価Grading4: 備考　Note The course has the following three sets of requirements:  1. Regular class attendance and active participation in class. 2. One to three comment(s) or questions based on a critical analysis of the required readings in each class. 3. One media content analysis paper and presentation.  Students will conduct a comparative media content analysis in which they examine various newspapers’ coverage of their chosen topic and give a presentation that demonstrates their understanding of the topic and their mastery of analytical abilities in the study of politics.  Course grades will be calculated in the following manner: Class participation       30% One to three comments or questions in each class         30% One media analysis paper  20% One media analysis presentation                          20%
備考Note0: ＊若干回休講、補講を行う可能性があります。補講を行う場合は、学部指定の補講日、もしくは講師と受講生の双方に都合のよい日と時間帯に、相談の上決定し実施します。There may be some class cancellations and make-up classes subsequently.  Make-up classes would be held either on days designated by the school or on mutually convenient days/periods, which would be decided upon consultations between the lecturer and students.

name: 【120】Introduction to Political Science／Introduction to Political Science [EN]
成績評価備考:
備考　Note
The course has the following three sets of requirements:

1. Regular class attendance and active participation in class.
2. One to three comment(s) or questions based on a critical analysis of the required readings in each class.
3. One media content analysis paper and presentation. Students will conduct a comparative media content analysis in which they examine various newspapers’ coverage of their chosen topic and give a presentation that demonstrates their understanding of the topic and their mastery of analytical abilities in the study of politics.

Course grades will be calculated in the following manner:

Class participation 30%
One to three comments or questions in each class 30%
One media analysis paper 20%
One media analysis presentation 20%
